response.justification: Bogdan brings genuinely relevant AI-native experience — current work on MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure at re:cinq matches the role's baseline expectation — plus real high-throughput systems exposure from Sociomantic's microservices handling millions of requests per second. However, Python, a must-have, appears nowhere on the resume (his systems work is in DLang, a niche language), his distributed-systems depth dates to 2018-2020, and there is little evidence of directing technical strategy at staff scope or managing cross-functional stakeholders in larger organizations. Worth a conversation to probe Python transferability and strategic scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling experience in his current role — directly matches the AI-native engineering environment
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong TypeScript/Node.js across recent roles, exceeding the working-level requirement, with a solid craftsmanship record (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development)
concerns: No Python listed anywhere on the resume — a stated must-have; his systems work is in DLang, which is niche and less transferable to the team's stack
Limited evidence of staff-level technical strategy, decision-making at scale, or cross-functional stakeholder management; most roles are senior IC at small companies
Core distributed-systems experience is 5+ years old; recent roles are product-focused full-stack work
